name: Galaxy Ace 2

flash_steps.name: Download firmware
flash_steps.description: Download or build the selected firmware image

flash_steps.name: Verify integrity
flash_steps.description: Check SHA256 hash of downloaded image

flash_steps.name: Backup current firmware
flash_steps.description: Back up current partitions via ADB before flashing

flash_steps.name: Flash U-Boot bootloader
flash_steps.description: Flash U-Boot stemmy to the boot partition. U-Boot replaces Samsung's secondary bootloader and provides fastboot mode for loading the mainline Linux kernel. This is the first step for any pmOS install.

flash_steps.name: Flash rootfs to USERDATA
flash_steps.description: Flash the postmarketOS rootfs image to the USERDATA partition. On the Ace 2, USERDATA is the largest partition (~2GB) and is used as the root filesystem for pmOS.

flash_steps.name: Flash kernel
flash_steps.description: Flash the pmOS boot image (kernel + initramfs + DTB) to the RECOVERY partition. U-Boot loads the kernel from this partition.

flash_steps.name: Reboot into Bender
flash_steps.description: Remove USB cable, then hold Volume Up + Home + Power for 3 seconds to boot. U-Boot will load the mainline kernel from RECOVERY, which mounts rootfs from USERDATA.

post_flash.name: Connect to WiFi
post_flash.description: On first boot, connect via USB networking (SSH to 172.16.42.1) and configure WiFi with connmanctl. Once connected, Bender's web UI is available at http://bender.local:8080 or the device's IP.

post_flash.name: Set up API key
post_flash.description: Open http://bender.local:8080 in a browser (or on the device screen if display patches are applied). Paste your Anthropic API key or scan a QR code. Bender will introduce itself and ask what you want to use the device for.

notes:
NovaThor U8500 phone with 768MB RAM and 4GB storage. Tight but viable for Bender — the agent server runs locally, inference is cloud-side.
postmarketOS support is at "testing" tier. OSmosis provides kernel patches for display (MCDE DPI + WS2401 panel) and audio (MSP I2S + AB8500 codec) that are not yet upstream.
Boot chain: Samsung PBL → U-Boot stemmy → mainline kernel (6.x) CAVEAT: U-Boot path failed on our test unit (SBL ignores Kernel PIT partition). Android app route is the current viable path.
Enter Download Mode: Volume Down + Home + Power. Two panel variants exist (LMS380KF01 90%, S6D27A1 10%) — U-Boot auto-detects via Samsung's lcdtype ATAG.
GPS chip: Broadcom BCM4751 (broken in pmOS). NFC: PNX544, only on GT-I8160P variant (antenna in back plate).

variants.notes: Has NFC (PNX544 chip, antenna in back plate)

known_issues.description: If the battery fully depletes, the device cannot charge because it cannot boot to the kernel, and it cannot boot if battery is too low (shuts down before reaching kernel). This is also the case on Android. Recovery requires an external battery charger or a replacement battery.
Bluetooth has no device address set after boot. Must manually configure: apk add bluez-btmgmt && btmgmt public-addr <bdaddr>
Phosh may have wrong DSI scaling, making the lockscreen unusable. Fix: set scale = 1 in /usr/share/phosh/phoc.ini under [output:DSI-1].
USB networking only works if USB is plugged in while "postmarketOS Loading..." is shown. Timing-sensitive.
All Codina variants use PRCMU firmware "U8420" that caps CPU to ~800MHz instead of the ~1GHz the SoC supports. Likely a pure software restriction.

Real-world testing on one GT-I8160 unit showed the SBL ignores the Kernel PIT partition entirely — flashing U-Boot, mainline zImage, or garbage to it has zero effect. SBL partition is read-only (Heimdall rejects writes). Secure boot fuses are NOT blown; the issue is partition mapping. The pmOS wiki U-Boot path may work on other units but failed on ours. Android app route is the viable alternative.

sensors: Amstaos TMD2672 light and proximity sensor

name: Galaxy Note II

flash_steps.name: Flash Replicant Recovery
flash_steps.description: Flash Replicant's own recovery (required for Replicant ROMs)

flash_steps.name: Flash ROM via sideload
flash_steps.description: Sideload Replicant via ADB in Replicant Recovery

notes: Exynos 4412 phone. Replicant requires its own recovery (not TWRP). Stock recovery will reject unsigned ZIPs. Enter Download Mode with Volume Down + Home + Power.
